title: Directions
description: Initially, Snuke can’t move at all. There are n tickets, and the price of the i-th ticket is pi. If Snuke buys the i-th ticket, for all points (x, y) and a nonnegative number t, he can move from (x, y) to (x + tai, y + tbi). Snuke wants to buy tickets and he wants to be able to travel between any two points. Compute the minimal possible total price of the tickets he must buy.
input: First line of the input contains one integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 2 · 105). Then n lines follow; i’th of these lines contains three integers ai, bi, pi (−109 ≤ ai, bi ≤ 109, 1 ≤ pi ≤ 109).
output: Print the minimal possible total price of the tickets he must buy in order to be able to move between any two points. If this is impossible, print −1 instead.
hint: In the Sample 1 you can, for example, buy tickets 1, 3, 6.
source: Contest, ICPC Japanese Alumni Group, JAG Summer Camp, JAG Summer Camp 2014 Day 2, Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Winter 2015, Day 2: Makoto Soejima Contest 1, Japanese Grand Prix, Contest, Open Cup, 2014/2015 Season, Stage 6: Grand Prix of Japan
